name: Qwen-Image
tagline: The open image model that can actually spell
description: A 20B image foundation model spanning text-to-image and precise instruction-based editing. Its signature strengths: complex text rendering — long passages, real typography, Chinese included — and identity-preserving edits. The 2.0 release added native 2K output and multi-image composition.
why: Ranked the strongest open image model at release and fully Apache-2.0 including weights — the clearest no-asterisks frontier image model of 2026, and the best open choice when the image needs legible words in it.
useCases: Poster and design generation with real text
Multilingual creative assets and LoRA fine-tuning
audience: Designers, developers shipping image features commercially, fine-tuners

setup.requirements: Diffusers pipeline or ComfyUI; heavy at full precision, but FP8 and offloading bring it down to ~4GB VRAM at reduced speed

limitations: A 20B model is heavy at full precision, and editing stability benefits from prompt rewriting.
take: Typography was the tell that gave AI images away. That tell is gone, and the model that killed it is free.
